purpose: Hand-checked candidate bridges from kernel-v0 (54 Explicated profile-1 concepts) and molecules-v0 into the onto-framenet tier. ANNOTATION-layer correspondences, NOT identity claims. The bridge here is unusually principled: a FrameNet frame's CORE frame elements are an explicit valency (argument) structure, which is exactly what a profile-1 relational-schema explication encodes as referents. So each link states 'this kernel concept evokes this frame, and its arguments correspond to these Core FEs'. Per-link confidence + caveat. Nothing endorsed; nothing enters the mapper.
method: manual review by the extracting agent (2026-07-07) against pinned onto-framenet records (FrameNet 1.7, CC BY 3.0). Only confident links listed; Core FEs quoted from the frame record.

alignments.note: kernel-v0:give (X gives Y to someone) <-> FrameNet Giving; the NSM valency X/Y/someone maps to Donor/Theme/Recipient. A textbook valency match.
kernel-v0:take <-> Taking (Agent/Theme/Source). Giving and Taking are Perspective_on/related converses in FrameNet, mirroring kernel-v0's give/take pairing.

kernel-v0:cause (because X happens, Y happens) <-> Causation (Cause/Effect). FrameNet's Causative_of frame-relation type also encodes the causative-of-a-state pattern kernel-v0 uses for break->broken.
kernel-v0:help (because X does something, Y can do what Y wants) <-> Assistance; Helper/Benefited_party/Goal map to X/Y/(what Y wants to do).
kernel-v0:promise (speech act) <-> Commitment (Speaker/Addressee/Message). The speech-act valency matches directly.
kernel-v0:lie (the words) <-> Prevarication (Speaker asserts a false Message). FrameNet Prevarication inherits from Statement (#43), giving the genus.

kernel-v0:believe is a propositional attitude (Cognizer + Content); closest FrameNet frames are Becoming_aware / Certainty / Opinion. Cognizer<->X, Phenomenon/Content<->the something believed. Medium: FrameNet splits belief across several frames.

kernel-v0:learn <-> the Student side of Education_teaching (Student/Subject); kernel-v0:teacher <-> the Teacher FE. FrameNet bundles teach+learn in one frame, whereas kernel-v0 separates learn (the event) and teacher (the role).

kernel-v0:remember (X knew Y before; because X knew it, X knows it now) <-> Remembering_information (Cognizer/Mental_content). kernel-v0:forget is the negative pole FrameNet does not lexicalize as one frame.

kernel-v0:find (before X did not know where Y is; because X did something, X knows now) <-> Locating (Perceiver/Sought_entity). lose is the converse (unknowing the location), unlexicalized as a single frame.

kernel-v0:break (X does something to Y; because of this, something happens to Y) <-> Cause_to_fragment or Render_nonfunctional (#416, Agent/Artifact). broken <-> the resultant non-operational state (Being_in_operation #880, negated). The break/broken causative->state pair aligns with FrameNet's Causative_of/Inchoative_of relation types.
Locomotion molecules <-> Self_motion (Self_mover + path FEs). FrameNet's rich path/goal FEs are peripheral-valency the NSM molecule leaves implicit.
molecule sleep <-> Sleep (one Core FE, Sleeper).

notes: See README.md for the full valency-mapping analysis. Headline: FrameNet's 3,501 Core frame elements over 1,221 frames are a large curated inventory of situation-type ARGUMENT STRUCTURE with role labels and cross-frame role mappings (12,393 FE-to-FE maps) — the bulk source that most directly encodes profile-1's referent/valency thinking. A frame + its Core FEs is an argument-structure axiom and an UPGRADE CANDIDATE toward a profile-1 relational-schema explication (the FEs give the referent slots; the NSM body must still be authored by hand — filed).
